title: Quake Champions

gpu: X.Org Gallium 0.4 on AMD TONGA (DRM 3.8.0 / 4.9.0-8-amd64, LLVM 3.9.1)
title: Quake Champions

notes: The only major issues I had with the game is that I ran into a lot of stuttering. Besides the stuttering just about everything else works great. Others have mentioned the stuttering comes from caching shaders, so maybe I have to play the game for longer but so far its almost perfect
title: Quake Champions

notes:
The game will freeze when loading any asset for the first time as it builds up a shader cache, nothing new there. Disabling Esync solved the mouse skipping issues I experienced and removed the stuttering I continued experiencing after shader cache generation, as well as boosted the framerate, absolutely essential tweak to play the game.

Performance is roughly 70-80% of Windows performance at ultra settings, haven't experienced any crashing as of late and any other technical issues encountered are also present on Windows.
title: Quake Champions

notes:
There was a noticeable improvement after disabling Esync, so I would advise you to disable it. Other then that, at the start of the map, the texture buffers must load which causes a serious drop in performance for about a minute or two. There are some occasional stutters after that though, even when running on low quality.

Overall performance is passable, but it won't be able to shine line some other games using Proton.
title: Quake Champions

notes: Ran a little slow with esync enabled, but with esync turned off, the game runs smoothly.
title: Quake Champions

notes:
Everything in the game itself works. Alt+Tab doesn't work. Switching/moving to another workspace usually works, but sometimes crashes the game.
The game itself runs stable at kinda acceptable FPS (~140 in Windows, ~60-70 via Proton). Sometimes FPS dip down to 20-30. Not quite sure in which situations, probably when there a a lot of effects while being in an infight. Disabling Esync seems to made it a little bit better, but not sure about that.
Although very stable, only silver rating as Quake Champions as a competitive FPS requires more than 60-70 FPS with stutters to play. If your PC is very powerful, it might be gold or even platinum for you.
title: Quake Champions

title: Quake Champions

notes:
Played the game without any tweaks for several hours. Had some stuttering at new maps, but overall it was a good experience.
Today i disabled Esync and i also found some tweaks, now quake runs smooth like hell:

PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% --set /Config/CONFIG/maxFpsValue 100 --set /Config/CONFIG/isLowResParticles 1 --set /Config/CONFIG/isLowViolence 1

(Tweaks: https://steamcommunity.com/app/611500/discussions/0/1471969431592752707/)
